reasoning: The failure was traced to severe packet loss on the F1-u interface (downlink), which was identified as the primary root cause indicator with the highest probability candidate. While multiple interfaces (F1-c, E2, and A1) and components (O-DU, O-CU, Near-RT RIC, and Non-RT RIC) were affected, the diagnostic reasoning indicates that the degradation in the F1-u user plane is the primary failure point, and the issues on other interfaces are symptomatic noise resulting from telemetry alerts and RIC policy applications. The failure chain starts with `f1_u_packet_loss_dl_percentage` affecting the F1-u interface, leading to connectivity failures in the O-CU and O-DU, and subsequently propagating telemetry to the Near-RT RIC and Non-RT RIC via the E2 and A1 interfaces.
actionable_steps: 1. Perform a physical layer audit of the transport network connecting the O-CU and O-DU to check for cable faults or hardware degradation. 2. Verify the MTU settings and VLAN configurations on the F1-u interface to ensure no packet fragmentation is causing the loss. 3. Inspect the O-DU and O-CU interface logs for buffer overflows or hardware errors on the network interface cards (NICs).
